summary: MCPace now has a name-minimized evidence pipeline: static metadata triage for strong cases, live safe probe for weak configured servers, conservative plan-only/blocked handling for untrusted or high-risk packages, and evidence scoring in profileEvidence output. Full policy widening still requires probe evidence on the target machine because random third-party packages must not be executed blindly.

random500Evidence.coreFinding: Package metadata alone is useful for fast static triage, but weak/unknown or medium-confidence records must be escalated to safe initialize + tools/list probes before relaxing concurrency.

evidencePipeline.purpose: find candidate and choose launcher; never relax concurrency from name alone

evidencePipeline.source: transport, package shape, entrypoint, env/header requirements, dependency family, file list, README/description

evidencePipeline.purpose: confirm MCP compatibility and actual tool surface without tools/call

evidencePipeline.source: annotations + title/description + inputSchema/outputSchema parser + declarative toolPolicies
evidencePipeline.trust: mixed; annotations are advisory unless trusted

evidencePipeline.source: package version/integrity + registry metadata hash + tools/list fingerprint
evidencePipeline.purpose: downgrade policy when server surface changes

done: mcpace advanced dev lab probe wrapper for safe initialize + tools/list probing
tool schema advisory parser for path/sql/url/credential/command/mutation signals
release hygiene: no node_modules, no downloaded package archives, no sandbox paths
requiresTargetMachineEvidence: actual live probe of untrusted user-selected servers
Rust cargo fmt/clippy/test/build in a Rust-capable environment
non-npm random sweeps when package registries are reachable
